full_response:
The worldline of an observer on the edge of a relativistic merry-go-round rotating clockwise with constant angular velocity \(\omega\) in an inertial frame is derived using standard kinematics. In cylindrical coordinates \((t, r, \theta, z)\) centered on the rotation axis, with \(\theta\) increasing counterclockwise, the observer remains at a fixed radius \(R\) and moves in the plane \(z = 0\). The angular position decreases linearly with time due to clockwise rotation.

\[
\boxed{r(t) = R,\quad \theta(t) = \theta_0 - \omega t,\quad z(t) = 0}
\]

Here, \(\theta_0\) is the angular position at \(t = 0\). If the observer starts on the positive \(x\)-axis (\(\theta_0 = 0\)), then \(\theta(t) = -\omega t\).

Confidence: 100%
